faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.supportText: On the support screen, you can find answers to all your questions about the app.

q1.question: How does the app work?
q1.answer: Linfedemapp is a tool developed for healthcare professionals to optimize the follow-up of patients with lymphedema. It allows recording anamnesis data, performing perimetry measurements, calculating limb volume, and tracking treatment progress through a detailed history.

q2.question: How is volumetry calculated in the app?
q2.answer:
Volume calculation in the app is performed using circumference measurements obtained by perimetry, using the indirect volumetry method. In this process, the limb is divided into successive segments of approximately 10 cm, each considered as a truncated cone. The volume of each segment is calculated by the formula:

V = (h / 12π) × (C1² + C1 × C2 + C2²)

where h corresponds to the segment height (distance between measurement points), and C₁ and C₂ are the proximal and distal circumferences, respectively. The sum of all segment volumes gives the total limb volume.

This method, described in the clinical guidelines of the American Physical Therapy Association, is recognized as a valid and reliable technique for estimating limb volume in patients with lymphedema (Levenhagen et al., 2017).

Reference:
LEVENHAGEN, Kimberly et al. Diagnosis of upper quadrant lymphedema secondary to cancer: clinical practice guideline from the Oncology Section of the American Physical Therapy Association. Physical Therapy, v. 97, n. 7, p. 729-745, 2017.
https://doi.org/10.1093/ptj/pzx050

q3.question: Is it possible to compare patient progress?
q3.answer: Yes. The 'Exam History' section stores all assessments. By selecting a patient, you can view the progression of limb volume, the percentage difference between assessments, and compare perimetry data over time, facilitating the analysis of treatment effectiveness.

q4.question: How is patient data protected?
q4.answer: Security is our priority. All data is transmitted via HTTPS and stored with encryption. Access is restricted and follows the guidelines of the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), ensuring the confidentiality of clinical information.

q5.question: How does report export work?
q5.answer: From the history, any exam can be exported as a PDF report. The document includes patient data, perimetry measurements, calculated volume, and evaluation date, ready to be attached to the electronic medical record or shared.

q6.question: How can I reset my password?
q6.answer: On the login screen, click on 'Forgot your password?'. You will need to provide your registered e-mail to receive a verification code and instructions to create a new password.

volumeAnalysis.noChanges: The limb presents a volume difference of {difference} cm³, but shows no volumetric changes suggestive of lymphedema.
volumeAnalysis.stage0: The limb presents a volume difference of {difference} cm³ and volume changes of {percentage}%, which may suggest stage 0 or subclinical lymphedema.
volumeAnalysis.stage1: The limb presents a volume difference of {difference} cm³ and volume changes of {percentage}%, suggesting stage I or mild lymphedema.
volumeAnalysis.stage2: The limb presents a volume difference of {difference} cm³ and volume changes of {percentage}%, suggesting stage II or moderate lymphedema.
volumeAnalysis.stage3: The limb presents a volume difference of {difference} cm³ and volume changes of {percentage}%, suggesting stage III or advanced lymphedema.
